meta.index.title: Encounty - Auto Shiny Counter for Pokémon
meta.index.desc: Encounty is a modern, open-source auto shiny counter for Pokémon shiny hunting. GPU-accelerated encounter detection, 60+ hunt methods, OBS overlays, all games Gen 1-9.
meta.update.title: Update & Install - Encounty
meta.update.desc: Download and update Encounty on macOS, Windows and Linux. Simple step-by-step instructions with direct downloads, no GitHub detour.
meta.changelog.title: Changelog - Encounty
meta.changelog.desc: Full release history for Encounty, the open-source auto shiny counter for Pokémon shiny hunting.
meta.testing.title: Detection Testing - Encounty
meta.testing.desc: How Encounty's encounter detection is tested: real gameplay recordings from 2D and 3D Pokémon games, strict pass criteria, current results.

hero.tagline: The modern, open-source auto shiny counter for Pokémon shiny hunting. Capture your game, let the GPU detect encounters, and never click a tally counter again.

features.heading: More features
features.gpu.title: GPU-accelerated auto-detection
features.games.title: All games, Gen 1-9
features.games.desc: Every mainline Pokémon game with game-specific shiny odds, including the Shiny Charm and accurate per-method probabilities.
features.methods.title: 60+ hunt methods
features.methods.desc: Masuda, Poké Radar, SOS chaining, DexNav, Mass Outbreaks, Sandwich hunts and many more, each with its own odds model.
features.parallel.title: Unlimited parallel hunts
features.overlay.title: OBS overlay editor
features.overlay.desc: Show your counters on stream: drag-and-drop overlay editor with live preview and a browser-source URL for OBS.
features.hysteresis.title: No double counting
features.hysteresis.desc: A three-phase state machine (hysteresis, cooldown, counting) with tolerance for single shaky frames makes sure each encounter is counted exactly once.
features.replay.title: Replay-based screenshots
features.stability.title: Automatic stability analysis
features.hotkeys.title: Global hotkeys
features.hotkeys.desc: Count, pause or correct manually from anywhere with system-wide hotkeys.
features.export.title: Template import & export
features.export.desc: Share your detection templates or move them between machines with one click.
features.dex.title: Pokédex with sprites
features.dex.desc: Built-in Pokédex search with multiple sprite styles for your hunts and overlays.
features.arch.title: Modern architecture
features.arch.desc: Go backend, React frontend and an Electron shell: fast, robust and actively developed.
features.platforms.title: Cross-platform & multi-arch
features.platforms.desc: Linux, Windows 11 and macOS, on x64 and ARM64. Coming soon to your Toaster™.
features.oss.title: Open source
features.langs.title: UI in 5 languages
features.langs.desc: English, German, Spanish, French and Japanese.
compare.heading: Why Encounty?
compare.text: A classic tally counter app only stores a number. Encounty watches your game and counts for you. Here is what that difference looks like.
compare.tableCaption: Feature comparison between Encounty and typical counter apps
compare.rowOdds: Accurate odds for every game & method
compare.proofPre: Detection is verified against real gameplay recordings, 2D and 3D.
how.heading: How it works
how.step1.title: Create a hunt
how.step1.desc: Choose your game, target Pokémon and hunt method. Encounty knows the correct odds.
how.step2.title: Capture your game
how.step2.desc: Select the game window or screen. Mark the encounter with a template, once.
how.step3.title: Hunt

testing.hero.title: How detection is tested
testing.hero.text: No lab numbers, no marketing benchmark: before every release, the detection pipeline has to prove itself against real recordings from real hunts, 2D and 3D.
testing.method.heading: The method
testing.method.step1.title: Real recordings
testing.method.step1.desc: The test set is real gameplay footage across five game generations, from Fire Red/Leaf Green (2D) to Sword/Shield and Scarlet/Violet (3D): soft resets, starters, fishing, breeding and fleeing wild encounters.
testing.method.step2.title: Templates like a user makes them
testing.method.step2.desc: For every recording, detection templates are created exactly the way a user would create them in the app, no hand-tuned special settings.
testing.method.step3.title: Hard pass criteria
testing.method.step3.desc: Every frame of every clip is scored. The suite only passes if every real encounter is found, the full video scan counts the exact number of encounters, and nothing is ever counted twice. The encounter simulation follows the app's real adaptive polling.
testing.credit: The test recordings were kindly provided by LEOsMIND. Huge thanks, this footage has substantially improved Encounty's development.
testing.results.heading: Current results
testing.results.error: The latest results could not be loaded right now.
testing.results.hwNote: Detection accuracy is hardware independent: the same scoring math returns the same result on any machine. This suite also runs entirely without a GPU, using the CPU pipeline.
testing.gap.heading: Signal vs. noise per scenario
testing.gap.text: For every scenario, the weakest real encounter frame must still score clearly above the strongest non-encounter frame. The distance between the two bars is the safety margin.
testing.parity.heading: Same result on CPU and GPU
testing.parity.text: The WebGPU shader pipeline is checked against the CPU pipeline on the same frames from the same recordings. Both must agree, so it does not matter which one your machine ends up using.
testing.coverage.heading: What gets tested
testing.repoNote: The test recordings contain game footage and therefore cannot be published; the method and the latest results are documented here instead, and the suite runs in CI against the privately stored fixtures. Questions or want to dig deeper?
faq.heading: FAQ
faq.llm.q: Was Encounty built with the help of AI?
faq.llm.a: Yes. Encounty is developed with the help of LLM coding assistants. A free application of this scope would simply not be possible as a solo project without them. Every change is reviewed, covered by automated tests and CI, and the complete source code is open on GitHub.
faq.free.a: Yes, completely. No ads, no pro tier, no paywall. Encounty is a hobby project, free under the AGPL-3.0 license.
faq.offline.q: Do I need an account or an internet connection?
faq.offline.a: No. Encounty runs entirely on your machine and works offline. Internet is only used for optional things like app updates and sprite downloads. Your hunts stay local.
faq.ban.q: Can using Encounty get me banned?
faq.ban.a: Encounty only watches: it reads your screen or capture card, never touches the game, sends no inputs and automates nothing. It is a smarter tally counter, not a cheat tool.
faq.games.a: Anything you can show on your screen: PC releases or a console through a capture card. Hunt tracking covers every mainline game from Gen 1 to 9, and auto-detection works wherever an encounter is visually recognizable.
faq.odds.q: Where does the odds data come from?
faq.odds.a: The odds and method data come from a community research thread on r/pokemon that documents the hunting methods of every game; the app credits this source under Settings, About.
faq.threed.q: Does detection work in 3D games too?
faq.threed.a: Yes. Before every release, detection has to pass automated tests against real gameplay recordings from 2D and 3D games, with strict criteria: exact encounter counts, no double counting.
faq.performance.q: How much performance does the detection cost?
faq.performance.a: Very little. Matching runs as WebGPU compute shaders on your GPU with adaptive polling, so the CPU stays free for your game. Systems without WebGPU fall back to a CPU pipeline automatically. An integrated GPU is enough.
update.hero.title: Get the latest Encounty
update.hero.text: Pick your system below for step-by-step instructions. I have preselected the one that matches your device, you can switch anytime.
update.mac.intro: Does Encounty update itself on macOS? No. On macOS you download the new version manually and drop it into your Applications folder. It only takes a minute.
update.mac.step1.title: Download the file
update.mac.step1.desc: Use the button above. Your browser saves Encounty-arm64.dmg to your Downloads folder.
update.mac.step2.title: Open the .dmg
update.mac.step2.desc: Double-click the downloaded file. A window opens showing the Encounty icon and your Applications folder.
update.mac.step3.title: Drag it into Applications
update.mac.step3.desc: Drag the Encounty icon onto the Applications folder. If macOS asks, choose Replace to overwrite your old version.
update.mac.damaged.title: If macOS says the app is damaged
update.mac.damaged.text: macOS may warn that Encounty is damaged or comes from an unidentified developer, and refuse to open it. Nothing is actually wrong: this happens because Encounty is free and open source and is not signed with a paid Apple certificate. Here are two ways to allow it.
update.mac.opt1.title: Option 1: run one command (recommended)
update.mac.opt1.text: Open the Terminal app and paste this command, then press Enter. It removes the quarantine flag macOS put on the download.
update.mac.opt2.title: Option 2: allow it in System Settings
update.mac.opt2.li1: Try to open Encounty once (the warning appears).
update.mac.opt2.li2: Open System Settings, then Privacy & Security.
update.mac.opt2.li3: Scroll down and click "Open Anyway" next to Encounty.
update.mac.safePre: This is safe: Encounty is open source and you can
update.mac.safeLink: read the full code on GitHub
update.win.intro: Want automatic updates? Install the Setup version once and Encounty keeps itself up to date from then on. No more manual downloads.
update.win.oneInstaller: One installer for every Windows PC: it works on both Intel/AMD (x64) and Windows on ARM (ARM64) and picks the right version automatically.
update.win.dlNoteSuffix: run it once, then Encounty updates itself.
update.win.step1.title: Download and run the Setup
update.win.step1.desc: Double-click the downloaded file. Windows installs Encounty for you.
update.win.step2.title: If Windows SmartScreen shows a blue "Windows protected your PC" box
update.win.step2.desc: Click "More info", then "Run anyway". SmartScreen warns because Encounty is a small open-source app without a paid code-signing certificate, not because anything is wrong.
update.win.step3.title: You are done
update.win.step3.desc: Future updates install themselves in the background. Just keep using Encounty.
update.win.portable.title: Using the portable version?
update.win.portable.text: The portable .exe does not install and does not update itself. To update, download the new portable file and replace your old one with it. Your hunts and settings are stored separately and stay intact. Like the installer, it runs on both x64 and ARM64.
update.win.portable.step1.title: Download the new portable .exe
update.win.portable.step1.desc: Use the button above. The same file works on any Windows PC.
update.win.portable.step2.title: Replace your old file
update.win.portable.step2.desc: Delete or overwrite the old Encounty .exe with the new one, then open it as usual.
update.linux.intro: Good news: the AppImage updates itself automatically. You only need to download it once. If you have not installed Encounty yet, grab it below.
update.linux.step1.title: Download the AppImage
update.linux.step1.desc: Use the button above for your architecture (x64 for most PCs).
update.linux.step2.title: Make it executable
update.linux.step2.desc: Right-click the file, then Properties, and allow "Execute", or run this in a terminal in the download folder:
update.linux.step3.title: Open it
update.linux.step3.desc: Double-click the AppImage to launch Encounty. From here on it keeps itself updated.
update.linux.libz.title: If it will not start (libz.so error)
update.linux.libz.textBefore: On a minimal Linux install Encounty may exit right away with a message like
update.linux.libz.textAfter: . The app needs the standard compression library "zlib", which some systems do not include by default. Installing the package below fixes it.
update.linux.libz.tableCaption: zlib package name and install command per Linux distribution

update.linux.libz.copyFedoraAria: Copy Fedora, RHEL or openSUSE command to clipboard
